name: eClinicalWorks Ambulatory EHR API
what_it_does: eClinicalWorks REST API for ambulatory EHR and practice management platform. Enables AI agents to access patient clinical records and encounter documentation, manage appointment scheduling and patient flow, process insurance billing and claims, access population health management data, retrieve quality measure and MIPS reporting data, handle telehealth session integration, manage patient portal communications, integrate with pharmacy and lab systems via eClinicalWorks network, and connect ambulatory data with hospital and specialty care partners.
use_cases: Accessing ambulatory clinical records and encounters from care coordination agents
Processing insurance billing and denial management from revenue cycle agents
Retrieving population health and quality measure data from MIPS reporting agents
Managing appointment scheduling and patient flow from practice operations agents
not_for: Hospital inpatient care without ambulatory clinic focus
Specialty-only practices where specialty EHRs (Modernizing Medicine, WebPT) are better fit
Non-clinical administration without clinical EHR documentation
best_when: An independent physician practice, FQHC, or medical group using eClinicalWorks wants AI agents to automate billing, scheduling, population health, quality reporting, and patient engagement.
avoid_when: HIPAA: Ambulatory clinical PHI. DOJ settlement history (2017 false claims) — verify current compliance posture. Revenue cycle automation requires billing compliance oversight. MIPS quality reporting requires clinical validation. BAA required.

auth.notes: eClinicalWorks uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. FHIR R4 for interoperability. Practice-level credentials with clinical, billing, and scheduling scopes. Developer portal with API documentation. Partner marketplace for integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — ambulatory EHR enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: Westborough, Massachusetts. Founded 1999. 180,000+ providers. 850,000+ appointments daily. Major ambulatory EHR. Privately held. 2017 DOJ $155M settlement for false claims (software defects). Rebuilt credibility post-settlement. Used heavily in FQHCs and independent practices. Competes with athenahealth and Epic Cheers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA: Ambulatory PHI — BAA required; 2017 DOJ settlement history — verify current compliance
No public MCP server — OAuth2 FHIR R4 + proprietary API via developer portal
No webhooks — polling required for appointment and clinical updates
FHIR R4 for interoperability — proprietary API for deeper operational access
MIPS/quality reporting automation requires clinical validation before submission
Population health management data is powerful but requires careful de-identification for aggregated analytics
agent_readiness.security_notes: Ambulatory PHI. HIPAA, SOC2, ONC. OAuth2. US data residency. BAA required. Historical DOJ settlement — verify current posture.

security_score_components.security_notes: Ambulatory PHI. HIPAA, SOC2, ONC. OAuth2. US data residency. BAA required. Historical settlement.
